Here's a paragraph about Astragalus:

Astragalus is a genus of flowering plants in the pea family (Fabaceae), commonly known as milkvetch. The roots of Astragalus have been used in traditional medicine for their health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Astragalus include saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Astragalus include capsules, tablets, and teas, which are used to treat conditions such as respiratory infections, cardiovascular disease, and digestive issues.
